outcome_contract.why: Local `.btn-primary` is a styling island: brand-token changes to hover shade, primary colour, or focus ring must be manually kept in sync between global.css and any DS-native button styles. Centralising on the DS Button means one source of truth.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: All caryina-owned call sites (12) use DS `<Button>` or an explicitly documented exception; `.btn-primary` CSS utility is removed from global.css once call sites are migrated.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: `.btn-primary` in global.css with explicit hover/active token references; DS Button uses opacity-based hover
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Hover colour divergence if passthrough className not matched exactly
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Yes — must define migration className that preserves token-based hover

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: 12 call sites with disabled, hover, active, focus-visible states; all use `.btn-primary` conventions
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: DS Button must expose all states via passthrough className; `disabled:opacity-50` is already standard
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Yes — verify disabled/loading states survive migration

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: No auth or sensitive data in button component
No analytics hooks on button elements
Render tests exist for all major pages with buttons
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: No visual regression tests; hover state untestable in JSDOM

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Button component swap has no runtime cost difference
12 call sites in one app; no feature flag needed; rollback = git revert
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: If visual regression is missed in code review, no automated catch
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Yes — plan should include visual spot-check in dev before commit

stage_payload.scope_summary: Caryina defines its own `.btn-primary` CSS utility in `apps/caryina/src/styles/global.css` rather than using the shared `<Button>` component from `@acme/design-system/shadcn`. This creates a styling island: any brand token change (primary colour, hover shade, radius) must be manually propagated both to the DS button definition and to the local class. This fact-find evaluates whether to adopt the DS Button across all 12 caryina call sites, what the visual delta would be, and what migration effort is required.
goals: Determine the exact visual difference between `.btn-primary` and DS `<Button>` with caryina tokens.
Establish which call sites are caryina-owned vs shared platform code.
Produce a migration approach that preserves visual fidelity or accepts a documented intentional change.
Decide scope: caryina-owned buttons only, or include platform-core AddToCartButton.
non_goals: Redesigning the button visual style beyond what is needed for faithful migration.
Migrating AddToCartButton in `@acme/platform-core` (shared across businesses — out of scope here).
Adding new DS Button variants or props not already present.
constraints: `@acme/ui/components/atoms/shadcn` import path is banned by ESLint `no-restricted-imports`. All DS imports must use `@acme/design-system/shadcn`.
`@acme/design-system` is already a declared dependency after dispatch 0002 (Input migration).
assumptions: DS `<Button>` with `compatibilityMode="passthrough"` renders the button element without adding its own bg/padding/radius classes, so caryina can provide explicit className overrides that fully control the visual output — matching the current `.btn-primary` appearance exactly.
`rounded-full` is universally applied alongside `.btn-primary` at every call site; this aligns with DS `shape="pill"` or equivalent className override.
key_entry_points: `packages/ui/src/components/atoms/shadcn/Button.tsx` — re-export from DS primitives (access restricted to `@acme/design-system/shadcn` import path)

rehearsal_issues.Scope Area: All 12 call sites enumerated and ownership confirmed
Hover colour divergence between local class and DS default
rehearsal_issues.Issues Found: [Advisory] Opacity-based hover differs from token-based hover

process_topology.note: None: local code path only. This change alters component imports and a CSS utility class. No CI/deploy lane, approval path, or operator runbook is affected.
